pages.raw_text:
The State Federation of Labor is
sending to all affiliated unions a
compiled statement showing the
vote of the members of the last
Washington legislature on all bills
in which labor was interested. On
the anti-boycott bill, considered the
fairest test vote of the senate, the
vote shows that Senators Bratt,
Graves and Rasher voted against
the passage of the bill and with
unions, while Senators Brown and
Henry voted for the bill and in the
interests of the corporations. In
the house, Bourse, Hoch, Huxtable,
Lindsey and Scott voted with the
worliingmen and against the bill,
while Crane. Fancher, Lindsey, Rat
cliffe and Doolittle voted for the
bill. The real purpose Of the bill
was to prevent under penalties the
placing of any firm on the unfair
list by the unions. Organized labor
took the position that passage of
the measure would have constituted
unfair discrimination against the
unions.
